deployment.evidence: Public code chirp-status-demo-1 created proof project 00fce81e-60b3-4ef1-b0b9-8c41e14e166e with web and official postgres-ssl:18 services, a 5 GB /var/lib/postgresql/data volume, generated 32-character secrets, no variable overrides or prompts, and terminal SUCCESS deployments.

ejection.evidence: Railway ejected proof service d2edd748-d4a4-4285-b794-fcce4d8d9ae5 into independent public repository lbliii/chirp-status-ejection-proof, rewired the source, and produced SUCCESS deployment f59573d9-42c5-4e42-a09c-6dc8ed228660 with PostgreSQL state intact.

restart.evidence: The proof web process drained and restarted on deployment 70f5d123-5413-45a7-bc7f-5bcf51fc6a91; readiness recovered and both Template persistence proof and Public HTTP proof remained visible from PostgreSQL.

rollback.evidence: Proof deployment 7121fa92-a36f-42a1-8880-f73d1771fef5 restored known-good source 577896e, passed readiness with both persisted monitors, then latest-source deployment d1e0cd6f-a959-4d90-84e7-9d78746156e9 restored c4f9888 successfully.

shutdown.evidence: Stopping proof deployment d1e0cd6f-a959-4d90-84e7-9d78746156e9 logged connection draining, lifespan shutdown, and server stop; ingress returned 404 until SUCCESS recovery deployment 147e34a5-80b6-4ce8-9aa4-d994aec55be2 restored readiness and persisted monitor state.

update.evidence: The zero-input proof advanced from v0.1.0 commit 577896e to contract-complete commit 3dde7df in SUCCESS deployment f1dddb4c-4bbc-45a5-8a7e-47eac6371001, applied migration 002_monitor_policy, passed maintenance/status/timeout live checks, and retained both monitors.
